methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Hexana is a seed-stage nuclear energy company that has raised €21M to date under founder Gabriel Lévy. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across the nuclear energy sector: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Standard peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private companies, and application of stage and valuation-tier filters yields fewer than three comparable datapoints—the minimum threshold for meaningful median extraction. While 8 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) produced a sufficiently robust subset. A fallback to all-sector median comparables would introduce material bias given the sector's heterogeneity in capital intensity, regulatory environment, and path to monetization.

Without sufficient same-stage or tier-matched peers, traditional comparable company analysis cannot be reliably triangulated for Hexana. Alternative valuation approaches—such as DCF analysis anchored to technology readiness, regulatory milestones, or capital requirements—would require operational and financial projections not present in the available data. The scarcity of early-stage nuclear energy comparables reflects both the sector's capital demands and relative newness of commercial deployment pathways. Any valuation estimate at this stage would rest primarily on individual transaction terms, post-money requirements from subsequent rounds, or cost-of-capital models rather than peer multiples.
